thesis: RTX is a top-tier defense prime contractor with a balanced defense/commercial aerospace portfolio. Strong defense spending tailwinds, record backlog exceeding $200B, and commercial aerospace recovery drive a BUY rating with margin expansion potential.

drivers: Accelerated U.S. and allied defense budgets drive record bookings at Raytheon
Pratt & Whitney GTF aftermarket ramps faster than expected with margin recovery
Collins Aerospace benefits from Boeing production recovery and Airbus rate hikes
Successful M&A or portfolio optimization unlocks shareholder value
Multiple expansion to 20x+ forward EPS as investors re-rate defense primes

assumptions: Steady execution across all three segments with mid-single-digit organic growth
Defense budgets grow at inflation-plus, maintaining backlog above $200B
Commercial aerospace recovery continues at a measured pace
Share buybacks and dividend growth support 8-10% annual EPS growth
Stock trades at 18-19x forward earnings, consistent with historical multiples

risks: U.S. defense budget cuts or program delays reduce Raytheon bookings
Commercial aerospace slowdown pressures Collins OEM and Pratt deliveries
Pratt & Whitney faces unexpected GTF technical issues or prolonged shop visit costs
Margin compression from fixed-price contract overruns or supply chain inflation
Multiple compression to 15x forward EPS on earnings stagnation

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — guidance update and margin trajectory
NDAA Appropriations Markup — defense budget direction
NATO Defense Ministers Meeting — replenishment commitments
Q3 2026 Earnings — commercial aerospace momentum check
FY2027 Defense Budget Request — top-line program visibility

key_risks: Defense budget volatility from geopolitical shifts or deficit reduction
Commercial aerospace cyclicality and Boeing production delays
Fixed-price development contract cost overrun risk
Export control and trade policy restrictions on international sales
